title: Elevate Government Affairs Internship 2026: Paid Full-Time Government Affairs Internship in Federal Policy, Congress, Transportation, Technology, and Energy Policy
summary: Are you passionate about federal policy, government relations, legislative affairs, transportation policy, technology regulation, telecommunications, or energy policy? Elevate Government Affairs is offering an exciting paid internship opportunity for aspiring government affairs professionals seeking hands-on experience in one of Washington s most dynamic policy environments. This full-time, paid, and in-person internship provides participants with the opportunity to work directly alongside experienced bipartisan government affairs professionals while gaining valuable exposure to congressional processes, policy analysis, client engagement, legislative tracking, and strategic advocacy. The internship is ideal for recent graduates, bachelor s degree holders, and emerging professionals who want to build a career in government affairs, public policy, lobbying, legislative affairs, political consulting, or public administration. Successful candidates must be available to begin work on August 31, 2026 , and remain in the role through December 18, 2026 . About Elevate Government Affairs Elevate Government Affairs was established by three highly experienced government relations professionals with a vision of helping organizations successfully navigate an increasingly complex government affairs landscape. The firm was created to address a growing challenge faced by organizations across sectors: ensuring their voices are heard in a policy environment that is becoming more competitive and complex. Elevate s mission centers on: Delivering measurable policy outcomes for clients Building customized government affairs strategies Providing personalized client engagement Creating innovative advocacy solutions Supporting clients at every level of government Leveraging bipartisan expertise to achieve policy goals What distinguishes Elevate Government Affairs is the depth of experience within its leadership and professional team. Collectively, the firm s professionals possess more than 125 years of combined experience working with: Members of the U.S. House of Representatives Members of the U.S. Senate Congressional Committees Executive Branch agencies Public policy organizations Government relations initiatives The organization emphasizes bipartisan collaboration and strategic innovation, enabling clients to effectively engage policymakers and decision-makers. Internship Overview This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in federal policy and government affairs while working directly with professionals who shape public policy strategies. Interns will become integral members of a collaborative team and contribute to projects involving: Transportation policy Infrastructure policy Technology policy Telecommunications policy Energy policy Congressional affairs Legislative analysis Client engagement The internship combines research, policy monitoring, communication, strategic analysis, and client support responsibilities. What Interns Will Do Participants will be actively involved in the day-to-day operations of the firm. 1. Policy Research and Analysis Interns will conduct detailed policy research to support client projects and internal strategic initiatives. Research responsibilities may include: Federal legislative analysis Regulatory developments Committee activities Executive Branch actions Policy trend monitoring Sector-specific issue research This experience helps interns develop a strong understanding of the policymaking process and current federal priorities. 2. Legislative Research Memoranda Interns will assist in preparing research memoranda that provide clients with valuable insights into legislative and policy developments. These documents may cover: Pending legislation Congressional priorities Committee activities Political developments Policy implications for clients Developing these materials strengthens research, writing, and analytical skills. 3. Client Deliverables Support Interns will contribute to the preparation of professional deliverables provided to clients. This may include: Policy updates Legislative summaries Briefing materials Strategic reports Issue analyses Business development documents The experience provides exposure to the consulting and government relations industries. 4. Major Legislation Tracking Monitoring legislative activity is a key component of government affairs work. Interns will assist with: Tracking bills in Congress Monitoring amendments Following committee actions Identifying legislative developments Preparing legislative status reports This responsibility provides firsthand exposure to the legislative process. 5. Congressional Hearing Coverage One of the most valuable aspects of the internship is the opportunity to follow congressional proceedings. Interns will: Monitor House hearings Monitor Senate hearings Draft hearing summaries Identify policy implications Prepare client-facing briefing documents This role enables participants to observe policymaking in action. 6. Client Meeting and Call Support Interns will participate in client communications and strategic discussions. Responsibilities include: Attending client calls Taking detailed meeting notes Tracking deliverables Documenting action items Supporting follow-up activities These experiences help develop professional communication and project management skills. 7. Business Development Assistance Interns may also support business development activities through preparation of: Proposal documents Pitch materials Strategic presentations Statements of work Client engagement materials This exposure provides valuable insight into the business side of government affairs consulting. Desired Qualifications Elevate Government Affairs is seeking motivated individuals who demonstrate a strong interest in public policy and government. Ideal candidates should possess: Strong Communication Skills Applicants should be capable of: Writing clearly and professionally Communicating effectively with colleagues and clients Synthesizing complex information Producing concise policy summaries Excellent Attention to Detail Government affairs work often requires precision and accuracy. Candidates should be comfortable: Reviewing legislative documents Managing multiple assignments Tracking policy developments Producing accurate deliverables Analytical Thinking Successful interns should be able to: Analyze policy developments Identify key legislative trends Evaluate policy implications Conduct independent research Organizational Skills Interns must be able to manage competing priorities while meeting deadlines. Knowledge of Government Structures Candidates should have at least a foundational understanding of: The U.S. Congress The House of Representatives The Senate Congressional Committees The Executive Branch Federal policymaking processes Desire to Learn Perhaps most importantly, successful applicants should demonstrate: Curiosity Initiative Professionalism Adaptability A willingness to learn new skills While previous congressional internship experience is preferred, it is not a requirement. Why This Internship Stands Out This opportunity offers significantly more than administrative internship experience. Participants gain: Real-world exposure to federal policymaking Direct interaction with government affairs professionals Congressional monitoring experience Legislative research skills Client-facing experience Professional writing experience Policy analysis training Networking opportunities Exposure to multiple policy sectors These experiences can serve as a strong foundation for careers in: Government affairs Public policy Legislative affairs Political consulting Public administration Advocacy Lobbying Public affairs Strategic communications Internship Benefits Key benefits of the internship include: Paid internship opportunity Full-time professional experience Direct exposure to federal policymaking Congressional hearing coverage experience Client engagement opportunities Legislative research experience Professional networking opportunities Mentorship from experienced government affairs professionals Career development in public policy and government relations Application Process Interested candidates should submit: Resume Cover Letter Applicants must combine both documents into a single PDF file and send them via email to: ethan@elevatega.com Candidates should ensure that application materials clearly demonstrate their interest in federal policy, government affairs, research, communication, and legislative processes. Important Dates Application Deadline: Not specified Internship Start Date: August 31, 2026 Internship End Date: December 18, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible since positions may be filled on a rolling basis. How This Opportunity Can Accelerate Your Career For aspiring policy professionals, few opportunities provide the combination of legislative exposure, client engagement, policy research, and professional development offered by Elevate Government Affairs. Whether your goal is to work in Congress, public affairs, advocacy, consulting, government agencies, or policy research, this internship can provide practical experience and professional connections that help launch a successful career in government and public policy. title: UNESCO Fellowships 2026: Over 450 Fully Funded International Research, Training, and Innovation Opportunities for Students, PhD Candidates, Young Professionals, and Project Leaders Worldwide
summary: UNESCO continues to empower the next generation of researchers, innovators, professionals, and changemakers through its extensive fellowship programmes. Supported by donors and extrabudgetary funding, UNESCO Fellowships provide financial assistance for research, academic studies, professional training, and innovative projects that contribute to UNESCO’s mission of advancing education, science, culture, communication, and sustainable development worldwide. With more than 450 fellowships available across multiple programmes, UNESCO offers one of the most diverse international fellowship portfolios for students, PhD candidates, postdoctoral researchers, early-career professionals, and project initiators seeking to expand their knowledge, strengthen their expertise, and contribute meaningful solutions to global challenges. These fellowships provide access to world-class institutions, international networks, advanced research opportunities, practical training experiences, and funding support that enable beneficiaries to make lasting impacts in their respective fields. Why UNESCO Fellowships Matter UNESCO fellowships are designed to foster knowledge creation, capacity development, international cooperation, and innovation. Through partnerships with governments, universities, research institutions, foundations, and private-sector organizations, UNESCO supports talented individuals who demonstrate the potential to contribute to sustainable development and positive societal transformation. The fellowships focus on: Advancing scientific research Supporting higher education Promoting international collaboration Encouraging innovation and entrepreneurship Strengthening professional skills Supporting youth leadership Preserving cultural heritage Advancing sustainable development goals Empowering underrepresented researchers and professionals Successful fellows gain valuable exposure to international research environments while developing skills and networks that can accelerate their careers. UNESCO Fellowships for Students and PhD Candidates UNESCO offers several fellowship opportunities specifically designed for students and doctoral researchers seeking advanced academic and research experiences. UNESCO/People’s Republic of China (The Great Wall) Fellowships One of UNESCO’s most established fellowship programmes, the Great Wall Fellowship supports advanced studies and research opportunities in China. Key highlights include: 75 fellowships available Advanced academic studies Research opportunities International cultural exchange Access to leading Chinese institutions Professional and academic development The programme strengthens educational cooperation while supporting future leaders and researchers from around the world. TWAS PhD Fellowships The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS), in partnership with UNESCO, provides opportunities for doctoral candidates pursuing advanced scientific research. Programme highlights: 160 fellowships available Support for PhD studies Advanced scientific research opportunities International collaboration Capacity building in science and technology These fellowships aim to strengthen scientific excellence and research capacity globally. OWSD PhD Fellowships The Organization for Women in Science for the Developing World (OWSD) supports women pursuing doctoral studies in scientific disciplines. The programme seeks to: Increase women’s participation in science Promote gender equality in research Support scientific innovation Strengthen leadership among women scientists UNESCO/ISEDC (Russian Federation) Co-Sponsored Fellowships Supported by the International Centre for Sustainable Energy Development, these fellowships focus on sustainable energy and related development challenges. Programme features: 20 fellowships available Specialized technical training Sustainable energy studies Capacity development Knowledge exchange opportunities Early Career Fellowships for Emerging Researchers and Professionals UNESCO recognizes the importance of supporting professionals during the early stages of their careers. UNESCO/People’s Republic of China (The Great Wall) Fellowships The Great Wall programme also offers opportunities for early-career professionals seeking specialized training and research experiences. UNESCO/Poland Co-Sponsored Fellowships in Engineering These fellowships support emerging engineering professionals and researchers. Highlights include: 28 fellowships available Advanced engineering training Research collaboration opportunities Professional development Access to Polish academic institutions UNESCO/Poland Co-Sponsored Fellowships in Archaeology and Conservation This specialized programme supports heritage preservation professionals. Key features: 7 fellowships available Archaeological research opportunities Conservation training Heritage management skills development International collaboration UNESCO/Republic of Korea Co-Sponsored Fellowships The Republic of Korea partners with UNESCO to support young professionals through advanced training opportunities. Programme highlights: 25 fellowships available Professional capacity building Research opportunities Technical training International networking UNESCO/Keizo Obuchi – Japan Young Researchers Fellowships Named after former Japanese Prime Minister Keizo Obuchi, this fellowship supports promising young researchers pursuing innovative studies that contribute to global development. Benefits may include: Research funding International recognition Academic mentorship Global networking opportunities UNESCO/Kingdoms Institute – Kingdom of Saudi Arabia AlUla Fellowships Programme This fellowship programme focuses on advancing research and innovation connected to heritage, archaeology, and cultural preservation. Highlights include: 10 fellowships available Specialized research support International collaboration Heritage and cultural studies opportunities TWAS Postdoctoral Fellowships TWAS supports researchers who have completed their doctoral studies and wish to pursue advanced scientific research. Programme highlights: 100 fellowships available Postdoctoral research opportunities Scientific capacity development International research collaboration OWSD Early Career Fellowship Designed specifically for women scientists, this programme supports research excellence and leadership development. The fellowship aims to: Strengthen research careers Support women in STEM fields Enhance scientific leadership Promote gender equity in research Silk Roads Youth Research Grant This initiative encourages young researchers to explore topics related to the historic Silk Roads and their contemporary relevance. Programme highlights: 12 fellowships available Youth-focused research support Intercultural dialogue promotion Historical and contemporary research opportunities Fellowships Supporting Innovative Projects UNESCO also supports individuals and teams working on transformative projects with the potential to address global challenges. Green Chemistry for Life – PhosAgro/UNESCO/IUPAC This programme promotes sustainable scientific innovation through green chemistry research. Key objectives include: Supporting environmentally friendly technologies Advancing sustainable chemistry Encouraging scientific innovation Addressing global environmental challenges UNESCO/Nestlé – Because Youth Matter This fellowship supports youth-led initiatives and innovative projects designed to create positive social impact. Programme highlights: 20 fellowships available Support for youth innovators Funding for impactful projects Leadership development opportunities Social entrepreneurship support Additional Fellowship Opportunities GEM Report Fellowship UNESCO also offers opportunities through the Global Education Monitoring (GEM) Report Fellowship, which supports professionals and researchers working on education-related issues and policy analysis. Participants may gain: Research experience Policy engagement opportunities International visibility Professional networking Benefits of UNESCO Fellowships Recipients of UNESCO Fellowships may benefit from: Financial support for research and studies International training opportunities Access to leading academic institutions Professional mentorship Global networking opportunities Enhanced career prospects Exposure to international best practices Research collaboration opportunities Leadership development Increased visibility and recognition How UNESCO Fellowships Contribute to Global Development UNESCO fellowships play a crucial role in supporting individuals who are working toward solutions in areas such as: Education Science and technology Sustainable development Climate action Cultural preservation Heritage conservation Innovation and entrepreneurship Gender equality Youth empowerment By investing in talented individuals, UNESCO contributes to building stronger institutions, advancing knowledge, and creating sustainable solutions that benefit communities worldwide. How to Apply Interested applicants should regularly monitor UNESCO’s fellowship portal and the specific programme pages for application openings, eligibility criteria, deadlines, required documentation, and selection procedures. Since each fellowship programme has its own application timeline and requirements, candidates are encouraged to review the details carefully before submitting an application. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post UNESCO Fellowships 2026: Over 450 Fully Funded International Research, Training, and Innovation Opportunities for Students, PhD Candidates, Young Professionals, and Project Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Horizon Europe Cluster 3 Civil Security for Society 2026: European Commission Opens Major Research Funding Opportunities with Deadline of 5 November 2026
summary: The European Commission, through the European Research Executive Agency (REA), has launched a major funding opportunity under Horizon Europe Cluster 3: Civil Security for Society. This initiative aims to strengthen Europe s capacity to address growing security challenges through cutting-edge research, innovation, and collaboration among researchers, institutions, public authorities, and industry stakeholders. With proposals due by 5 November 2026 , this funding programme offers organizations the opportunity to secure support for projects that contribute to crime prevention, counter-terrorism efforts, cybersecurity, disaster resilience, border management, and critical infrastructure protection. As global security threats continue to evolve, the Horizon Europe Cluster 3 programme seeks innovative solutions that enhance the safety, resilience, and security of societies across Europe and beyond. About Horizon Europe Cluster 3: Civil Security for Society Horizon Europe is the European Union s flagship research and innovation framework programme. Within this framework, Cluster 3 focuses specifically on Civil Security for Society. The programme responds to a broad range of contemporary security challenges, including: Cybercrime and cybersecurity threats Terrorism and organized crime Border security challenges Natural disasters Human-made disasters Protection of critical infrastructure Privacy and trust in digital systems Crisis management and emergency response Through strategic investments in research and innovation, the European Commission aims to support solutions that help build safer, more resilient, and more secure societies. Funding Areas Available in 2026 Applicants can submit proposals under several thematic areas within Cluster 3. 1. Better Protect the EU and its Citizens against Crime and Terrorism This funding area supports projects designed to: Prevent and combat crime Counter terrorism and radicalization Improve law enforcement capabilities Develop innovative security technologies Strengthen intelligence and investigative tools Enhance public safety measures Projects should contribute to a safer environment for European citizens while respecting fundamental rights and democratic values. 2. Effective Management of External Borders This topic supports innovative solutions that improve border management and security. Potential project areas include: Smart border technologies Border surveillance systems Identity management solutions Migration management support tools Risk assessment technologies Cross-border cooperation mechanisms The goal is to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of external border operations while ensuring compliance with legal and ethical standards. 3. Resilient Infrastructures Critical infrastructure forms the backbone of modern societies. This funding area supports projects focused on: Infrastructure resilience Protection of energy systems Transportation security Water supply protection Communication networks security Risk prevention and mitigation Infrastructure recovery and continuity planning Projects should strengthen society s ability to withstand and recover from disruptions and attacks. 4. A Disaster Resilient Society for Europe Natural disasters and emergencies continue to pose significant challenges worldwide. This funding stream supports projects aimed at: Disaster risk reduction Early warning systems Emergency preparedness Climate-related disaster resilience Crisis response technologies Community resilience initiatives Disaster recovery strategies Successful projects will contribute to improving Europe s preparedness and response capacity. 5. Strengthened Security Research and Innovation (SSRI) This thematic area focuses on strengthening Europe s overall security research ecosystem through: Research capacity building Innovation acceleration Knowledge transfer Stakeholder collaboration Policy support Security innovation ecosystems Technology adoption and scaling Projects are expected to bridge the gap between research findings and practical implementation. Deadline for Applications Application Deadline: 5 November 2026 Applicants are encouraged to begin consortium building and proposal preparation as early as possible due to the complexity and competitiveness of Horizon Europe funding opportunities. Who Should Consider Applying? This opportunity is particularly relevant for: Universities Research institutions Security agencies Public authorities Technology companies Cybersecurity firms Innovation hubs SMEs Civil society organizations Non-governmental organizations International research partnerships Disaster management agencies Border management authorities Law enforcement organizations Collaborative projects involving multiple organizations and countries are often encouraged under Horizon Europe. Support Available for Applicants The European Research Executive Agency provides extensive support to applicants throughout the proposal preparation process. Available resources include: Guidance on Building a Consortium Applicants can access support and recommendations on: Finding suitable project partners Creating multidisciplinary teams Building strong international collaborations Structuring effective consortia Application Process Guidance Prospective applicants can obtain information regarding: Proposal preparation Funding rules Eligible project types Evaluation criteria Submission procedures Open Science Requirements Applicants must ensure compliance with Horizon Europe principles relating to: Open access publishing Research data management Transparency and reproducibility Knowledge sharing Gender Equality Requirements Projects must address Horizon Europe requirements concerning: Gender equality considerations Inclusive research practices Gender-sensitive approaches where relevant Managing a Successful Horizon Europe Project Organizations that receive funding will need to effectively manage project implementation. The programme offers guidance on: Project Management Including: Grant agreement compliance Financial management Consortium coordination Deliverable management Reporting obligations Communication Activities Beneficiaries are expected to: Communicate project achievements Increase visibility of project outcomes Engage stakeholders Promote public awareness Dissemination and Exploitation Projects should develop strategies for: Sharing research findings Maximizing impact Supporting policy development Commercializing innovations where appropriate Policy Impact The programme also provides guidance on how project results can effectively inform policymakers and contribute to evidence-based decision-making. Why This Opportunity Matters Security challenges today are increasingly interconnected and complex. Cyber threats, organized crime, infrastructure vulnerabilities, climate-related disasters, and emerging technologies require innovative responses supported by robust research and collaboration. Through Horizon Europe Cluster 3, the European Union is investing in transformative ideas that can strengthen security, resilience, and societal well-being while fostering technological advancement and scientific excellence. For organizations working at the intersection of security, innovation, technology, and public policy, this funding opportunity provides a valuable platform to develop impactful solutions and contribute to a safer future. title: Fully Funded Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship 2027 in Japan: Six-Month Research Opportunity for Global Scholars
summary: Researchers seeking international collaboration, advanced research facilities, and meaningful academic exchange now have an exceptional opportunity to conduct fully funded research in Japan through the Matsumae International Foundation (MIF) Research Fellowship Program 2027. The prestigious fellowship supports outstanding non-Japanese researchers by providing financial support, research opportunities, and access to world-class Japanese universities and research institutions. Successful fellows will spend six months in Japan conducting research while building international partnerships and contributing to scientific advancement. The fellowship is designed to promote mutual understanding, academic cooperation, and long-term relationships between Japan and researchers from around the world. Applications for the 2027 fellowship cycle are currently open. About the Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship The Matsumae International Foundation (MIF) was established in 1979 by Dr. Shigeyoshi Matsumae, the founder of Tokai University. The foundation was created with a vision of promoting global peace and international friendship through academic exchange and research collaboration. For more than four decades, the fellowship has welcomed talented researchers from around the world to Japan, providing opportunities to undertake advanced research projects while fostering cultural understanding and international cooperation. The program enables researchers to work closely with Japanese academics and institutions, helping participants expand their expertise and strengthen their professional networks. Fellowship Duration Selected fellows will conduct research in Japan for: Six months Research activities may be undertaken during the fellowship period scheduled between June 2027 and March 2028. Fellowship Benefits The Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship is fully funded and offers a comprehensive support package. Benefits include: Monthly Allowance Fellows receive: JPY 220,000 per month This allowance is intended to cover living expenses and support research activities during the fellowship period. Arrival Grant Successful applicants receive: JPY 120,000 arrival allowance This funding assists fellows with initial settlement costs upon arrival in Japan. Round-Trip Airfare The fellowship covers: Economy-class airfare between the fellow s home country and Japan Insurance Coverage Participants receive: Overseas travel and medical insurance during their fellowship stay Study Tour Fellows may also participate in: Organized cultural and academic study tours in Japan These activities provide opportunities to learn more about Japanese society, culture, and research environments. Research Fields The fellowship welcomes research proposals from a broad range of academic disciplines. Priority is often given to research in: Natural Sciences Engineering Medicine Agriculture The program particularly values research that can contribute to solving important challenges facing society and support development in the fellow s home country. Why Apply for This Fellowship? Japan is home to some of the world s leading universities, laboratories, and research centers. Through this fellowship, researchers gain access to: Advanced Research Facilities Participants can work with cutting-edge technologies and infrastructure available at Japanese institutions. International Collaboration The fellowship creates opportunities to collaborate with leading Japanese researchers and establish long-term academic partnerships. Global Professional Network Fellows become part of an international network of researchers and alumni from around the world. Cultural Exchange Living and working in Japan provides a unique opportunity to experience Japanese culture while strengthening international understanding. Career Advancement The international experience, research outputs, and professional connections developed during the fellowship can significantly strengthen a researcher s academic career. Who Can Apply? Applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements before submitting an application. Nationality Requirement Applicants must: Be non-Japanese citizens Academic Qualification Applicants must: Hold a PhD degree at the time of application Host Institution Requirement Applicants must: Obtain acceptance from a host institution in Japan before applying The host institution may include: Universities National research institutes Public research organizations Equivalent academic institutions Language Requirement Applicants must demonstrate sufficient ability to conduct research in: English or Japanese Age Requirement Applicants should generally be: 45 years old or younger Employment Requirement Applicants must: Be employed in their home country Intend to return to their home country after completing the fellowship Application Process The fellowship requires applicants to secure a host institution before submitting an application. Step 1: Identify a Host Institution Applicants should contact professors or research groups in Japan whose work aligns with their research interests. Step 2: Obtain Host Acceptance Researchers must receive confirmation from a Japanese institution agreeing to host their proposed research project. Step 3: Prepare Application Materials Applicants should gather all required documentation, including research proposals and supporting materials. Step 4: Submit Application Applications must be submitted according to the instructions provided by the Matsumae International Foundation. Application Deadline Click here for more and to apply Deadline: 30 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to begin contacting potential host institutions as early as possible because obtaining host acceptance can take considerable time. Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Academic excellence Research achievements Quality and relevance of the research proposal Potential impact of the proposed research Feasibility of the project Contribution to international cooperation and academic exchange Important Tip for Applicants One of the most important components of a successful application is securing a strong host institution in Japan. Researchers should carefully identify professors whose expertise closely matches their proposed research and establish communication early. A well-aligned host institution can significantly strengthen the overall application. Why This Fellowship Stands Out Unlike many research funding opportunities, the Matsumae International Foundation Research Fellowship combines generous financial support with academic independence, international collaboration, and cultural exchange. For researchers seeking to advance their careers, access world-class facilities, and contribute to global scientific cooperation, this fellowship represents an outstanding opportunity to conduct meaningful research in one of the world s leading research environments. Interested applicants should begin preparing their applications and securing host institution support well before the deadline of 30 June 2026.
